# Missing Credentials — Story Agent

**Generated by:** Crew Security Review (Worf presiding)  
**Stardate:** 2026-06-07  
**Baseline client:** Client AG (`client-int`) — regulated tier (gold standard)

---

## ⚠️ Critical Missing Credentials (Client / Regulated Tier)

These credentials are **required before any Client-tier request can be processed**. The HTTP MCP endpoint will return `401 Unauthorized` until all are configured.

| Credential | Where to Set | SSM Path | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| `CLIENT_ENTRA_TENANT_ID` | AWS SSM | `/story-agent/client/entra-tenant-id` | Azure AD tenant ID for Client. Used to validate the `iss` claim in Entra Bearer tokens. |
| `CLIENT_ENTRA_AUDIENCE` | AWS SSM | `/story-agent/client/entra-audience` | Expected `aud` claim — the app registration client ID that was issued to this service. |
| `CLIENT_ENTRA_JWKS_URI` | AWS SSM | `/story-agent/client/entra-jwks-uri` | JWKS endpoint for Client's Entra tenant. Used for cryptographic token signature verification once the `jose` package is installed. |
| `REDIS_URL` | AWS SSM | `/story-agent/client/redis-url` | Redis connection string for per-session state isolation (`user-session-id` scoping). Without this, Client-tier session isolation cannot be enforced. |
| `SUPABASE_KEY` | AWS SSM | `/story-agent/client/supabase-key` | Supabase service role key. Required for writing to `sa_http_auth_audit` and `sa_mission_debriefs`. Must be the **service role** key (not anon). |
| `GITHUB_TOKEN` | AWS SSM | `/story-agent/client/github-token` | GitHub PAT or GitHub App installation token scoped to the `client-int` org. Required for `create_story_branch`, `open_pull_request`, `deliver_mission_output`. |

---

## ⚠️ Critical Missing Credentials (All Tiers / General Operation)

These are required for **any** story-agent operation, regardless of client tier.

| Credential | Env Var | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| Supabase URL | `SUPABASE_URL` | Supabase project URL. Shared with `ai-enterprise-os`. |
| Supabase Key | `SUPABASE_KEY` | Service role key. Used for all DB writes. |
| GitHub Token | `GITHUB_TOKEN` | For branch creation and PR management. |
| Aha domain | `AHA_DOMAIN` | Your Aha subdomain (e.g. `your-company.aha.io`). Required for `get_story`, `list_stories`, `update_aha_story_status`. |
| Aha API key | `AHA_API_KEY` | Aha API key. Found under **Profile → Developer → API Key**. |

---

## WorfGate Environment Variables

These control WorfGate's outbound security enforcement. **Client tier requires specific values.**

| Variable | Client-Required Value | Default | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| `WORFGATE_ENFORCE` | `"true"` | `"true"` | WorfGate hard enforcement mode. Must be `"true"` for Client. |
| `WORFGATE_ALLOWED_GITHUB_ORGS` | `"client-int"` | `""` | Comma-separated allowlist of GitHub orgs. Client commits only go to `client-int`. |
| `WORFGATE_ALLOW_CONTROLLED` | `"false"` | `"false"` | Whether controlled data can leave the system. Must be `"false"` for Client. |
| `WORFGATE_CONTROLLED_MARKERS` | *(see below)* | built-in defaults | Custom controlled-data markers. Leave unset to use Client policy from `ClientSecurityPolicy`. |

Client controlled-data markers (auto-applied when `x-client-id: client-int`):
`client`, `client-int`, `confidential`, `internal use only`, `regulated`, `customer data`, `patient`, `phi`, `pii`, `secret`, `proprietary`, `pharmaceutical`, `crop science`, `gdpr`, `restricted`

---

## HTTP MCP Server (StreamableHTTP Transport)

Required when running `STORY_AGENT_HTTP_PORT` to expose the HTTP MCP endpoint for cs-p3-agent integration.

| Variable | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `STORY_AGENT_HTTP_PORT` | Port for HTTP MCP endpoint (e.g. `3101`). If unset, HTTP transport is disabled; stdio only. |
| `CREW_HTTP_SIGNATURE_VERIFY` | Set to `"true"` to require full JWKS signature verification (needs `jose` package installed). Default `"false"` — validates claims only until `jose` is added. |

### To enable full cryptographic JWT verification:
```bash
pnpm add jose --filter @story-agent/mcp-server
```
Then update `packages/mcp-server/src/lib/http-auth-middleware.ts` — the JWKS verify stub at the comment `// NOTE: Full Entra JWKS verification requires a JWT library (e.g. jose)`.

---

## Chat Stream Endpoint (`/api/chat/stream`)

| Variable | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `CREW_FAST_MODE` | Default `"true"`. When true, only Picard+Data+Worf run per chat request (3 LLM calls). Set to `"false"` for full 11-crew analysis. |

---

## cs-p3-material-investigation-agent Integration

When registering story-agent as a second `PersistentMCPClient` in the cs-p3-agent, these additional credentials must be present in the cs-p3-agent's environment:

| Variable | Where (cs-p3 agent) | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `STORY_AGENT_MCP_URL` | AWS SSM | Full URL of story-agent's HTTP MCP endpoint (e.g. `http://story-agent-service:3101/mcp`) |
| Story agent Bearer token | AWS SSM | Service account token for cs-p3-agent→story-agent auth. Must match the audience expected by story-agent's auth middleware. |

---

## LLM Provider (Crew Analysis)

| Variable | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `CREW_LLM_PROVIDER` | `"demo"` (deterministic, no API calls) \| `"copilot"` \| `"approved"` (live Anthropic via approved gateway) |
| `CREW_LLM_MODEL_PROFILE` | `"cost_optimized"` (default) — critical crew → `global.anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-6`, support crew → `global.anthropic.claude-3-5-haiku` |

For `CREW_LLM_PROVIDER=approved`:

| Variable | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `APPROVED_LLM_ENDPOINT` | Base URL of the approved LLM gateway (same endpoint as cs-p3-material-investigation-agent) |
| `APPROVED_LLM_API_KEY` | API key / Bearer token for the approved LLM gateway |

---

## AWS SSM Setup (Client Regulated Tier)

For local development, export SSM values as environment variables. In production, the service should use an IAM role with SSM read access.

Required SSM paths (Client):
```
/story-agent/client/entra-tenant-id
/story-agent/client/entra-audience
/story-agent/client/entra-jwks-uri
/story-agent/client/redis-url
/story-agent/client/supabase-url
/story-agent/client/supabase-key
/story-agent/client/github-token
```

Populate with:
```bash
aws ssm put-parameter --name "/story-agent/client/entra-tenant-id" --value "<YOUR_TENANT_ID>" --type SecureString
aws ssm put-parameter --name "/story-agent/client/entra-audience"   --value "<YOUR_APP_CLIENT_ID>" --type SecureString
aws ssm put-parameter --name "/story-agent/client/entra-jwks-uri"   --value "https://login.microsoftonline.com/<tenant>/discovery/v2.0/keys" --type String
aws ssm put-parameter --name "/story-agent/client/redis-url"        --value "<REDIS_CONNECTION_STRING>" --type SecureString
aws ssm put-parameter --name "/story-agent/client/supabase-url"     --value "<SUPABASE_URL>" --type String
aws ssm put-parameter --name "/story-agent/client/supabase-key"     --value "<SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY>" --type SecureString
aws ssm put-parameter --name "/story-agent/client/github-token"     --value "<GITHUB_PAT>" --type SecureString
```

---

## Credential Validation at Startup

The MCP server logs all missing Client-tier credentials at startup:
```
[auth] ⚠️  Missing 3 credential(s) for Client-tier (regulated) security:
[auth]   - CLIENT_ENTRA_TENANT_ID (ssm) [SSM: /story-agent/client/entra-tenant-id]: ...
[auth]   - CLIENT_ENTRA_JWKS_URI (ssm) [SSM: /story-agent/client/entra-jwks-uri]: ...
[auth]   Client-tier requests will be rejected until all credentials are configured.
```

To check programmatically, call the MCP tool `crew_integrity_summary` or query `/api/crew/status`.

---

## Security Tier Summary

| Client | Tier | Entra Required | Session Isolation | WorfGate | SSM Required | GitHub Org |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `client-int` | **regulated** (gold standard) | ✅ Yes | ✅ Yes | 🔴 Hard | ✅ Yes | `client-int` |
| `familiarcat` | **enterprise** | ❌ Any OIDC | ✅ Yes | 🔴 Hard | ❌ No | `familiarcat` |
| *new enterprise client* | enterprise | ❌ Any OIDC | ✅ Yes | 🔴 Hard | Optional | (client org) |
| *new standard client* | standard | ❌ No | ❌ No | 🟡 Advisory | ❌ No | (any) |

> **Rule:** New clients never start at `standard` tier. The default for unknown clients is `enterprise`.  
> **Rule:** No requirement in the `regulated` (Client) tier can be lowered without explicit security review by Worf.  
> **Reference project:** `familiarcat/retailer-rewards-program` — enterprise tier reference implementation. React CRA app, loyalty point tracking, PII-lite data, no regulated obligations.

## Retailer Rewards Program (familiarcat) — Enterprise Tier

`git@github.com:familiarcat/retailer-rewards-program.git`

This project demonstrates the **enterprise tier** — below Client gold standard but above standard.

| Credential | Env Var | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| OIDC JWKS URI | `STORY_AGENT_AUTH_JWKS_URI` | Any OIDC provider (not required to be Entra) |
| OIDC Audience | `STORY_AGENT_AUTH_AUDIENCE` | Expected aud claim for familiarcat tokens |
| WorfGate enforce | `WORFGATE_ENFORCE=true` | Required — same as Client |
| GitHub org | `WORFGATE_ALLOWED_GITHUB_ORGS=familiarcat` | Commits only go to familiarcat repos |
| Redis | `REDIS_URL` | Session isolation (env var acceptable, no SSM mandate) |
| Supabase | `SUPABASE_URL` + `SUPABASE_KEY` | DB access (env var acceptable) |
| GitHub token | `GITHUB_TOKEN` | Scoped to familiarcat org |

**What differs from Client:**
- No Entra issuer requirement — any OIDC JWT accepted
- `controlledDataHardBlock=false` — can be relaxed per deployment
- No SSM mandate — env vars acceptable
- 2-hour session TTL (vs 1-hour for Client)
- WorfGate controlled markers include `transaction`, `rewards`, `loyalty` (domain-specific)

**Memory isolation:** All crew mission memories for `familiarcat/retailer-rewards-program` are stored with `client_id='familiarcat'` and will never surface in a Client session.
